item.title: Triple shooting, CDB interview, labor
item.ns0:title: One Dead and Two Hospitalized in Brazen Daylight Triple Shooting on Constitution Drive — Sunday, June 7th, 2026
item.description:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, a violent holiday weekend concludes with a brazen triple shooting on Constitution Drive as police investigate a series of murders and a suspicious death across three islands. We bring you an exclusive sit-down with the President of the Caribbean Development Bank on the region's battle for hurricane resilience and explore historic labor law reforms aimed at protecting the well-being of Bahamian workers. Our team also introduces you to a dedicated group of "Police Legends" caring for retired, shut-in colleagues and captures the vibrant energy of the Bahamas Masqueraders’ 11th-anniversary road march. Stay tuned for these headlines, a full weekend sports wrap-up, and your seven-day weather outlook to start your week.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, a violent holiday weekend concludes with a brazen triple shooting on Constitution Drive as police investigate a series of murders and a suspicious death across three islands. We bring you an exclusive sit-down with the President of the Caribbean Development Bank on the region's battle for hurricane resilience and explore historic labor law reforms aimed at protecting the well-being of Bahamian workers. Our team also introduces you to a dedicated group of "Police Legends" caring for retired, shut-in colleagues and captures the vibrant energy of the Bahamas Masqueraders’ 11th-anniversary road march. Stay tuned for these headlines, a full weekend sports wrap-up, and your seven-day weather outlook to start your week.
-----

item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, a violent holiday weekend concludes with a brazen triple shooting on Constitution Drive as police investigate a series of murders and a suspicious death across three islands. We bring you an exclusive sit-down with the President of the Caribbean Development Bank on the region's battle for hurricane resilience and explore historic labor law reforms aimed at protecting the well-being of Bahamian workers. Our team also introduces you to a dedicated group of "Police Legends" caring for retired, shut-in colleagues and captures the vibrant energy of the Bahamas Masqueraders’ 11th-anniversary road march. Stay tuned for these headlines, a full weekend sports wrap-up, and your seven-day weather outlook to start your week.
-----

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
